chief_complaint: A 60-year-old woman presents with progressive cough and exertional dyspnea.
history: She has a history of right breast carcinoma and has received chemotherapy, whole breast radiotherapy, and pembrolizumab. Symptoms developed during the sixth pembrolizumab cycle.
exam: Vital signs: normal. Physical examination: bilateral lung fields clear, no wheezing or crackles.
labs: CT of the thorax: right upper lobe consolidation with air bronchogram and surrounding ground-glass opacities. Bronchoalveolar lavage: excluded infection and malignancy. Spirometry: improvement within 3 months.
